initiatives.title: Analysis & Identification of Legacy Data (>5 Years) - 13909
initiatives.description:
Following the data retention policy of Surbo we need to accurately identify the data of all chatbot interaction data saved in S3  that will be older than 5 years as of 31 December 2025. This involves mapping data created on or before 31 December 2020.

S3 Path: surbo-s3prod/intents, surbo-s3prod/user_responses

A separate ticket will be provided for deletion of this identified data once this is done.

initiatives.title: Clickhouse Implementation for Analytics | Surbo - 13928
initiatives.description:
Currently, the Audience section displays user metrics such as All Users, New Users, and Daily Users. This functionality is currently enabled only for the PNB account and the data is stored using the Redis HyperLogLog data structure.

To improve accuracy, scalability, and reliability, we plan to migrate the storage and analytics of audience data to ClickHouse and enable this feature for all clients.

Current Scenario:

The Audience section shows the following metrics:

All Users

New Users

Daily Users

Data is currently stored using Redis HyperLogLog.

This feature is enabled only for the PNB account.

Redis-based implementation has limited accuracy and scalability for long-term analytics.

Proposed Implementation:

Enable the Audience analytics feature for all clients.

Replace Redis HyperLogLog storage with ClickHouse.

Surbo will store audience-related data in ClickHouse.

Analytics queries will retrieve the required metrics directly from ClickHouse.

Benefits:

Improved data accuracy.

Better scalability for large user volumes.

More reliable analytics infrastructure.

initiatives.title: Modularization of ML - 13839
initiatives.description:
Currently, the Surbo platform is deployed as a combined or tightly coupled system where multiple services are packaged and deployed together. This includes ML-related capabilities such as OCR, Generative AI, and Intent Detection.

This approach results in:

Deployment of ML services even when not required.

Higher infrastructure and compute costs.

Limited flexibility for client-specific deployments.

Increased operational complexity.


Problem Statement

The current architecture does not allow independent deployment of the ML service. As a result:

ML components are deployed even for clients not using AI features.

GPU/compute-heavy services increase infrastructure cost.

Requirement

Refactor the ML capabilities into a separate, independently deployable ML Service.

This service will include:

OCR processing

Generative AI capabilities

Intent detection engine

The ML service will:

Be deployed only where required.

Expose APIs for other Surbo modules.

Scale independently based on workload.

Operate as an optional module in each instance.


In Scope

Separation of ML components into an independent service.

API exposure for:

OCR

Generative AI

Intent Detection

Deployment configuration to enable or disable ML service per instance.

Integration of existing Surbo modules with the ML service via APIs.

Out of Scope

Modularization of non-ML services.

UI redesign.

Feature enhancements unrelated to ML service separation.

Business Justification

This change will:

Reduce infrastructure costs for non-AI deployments.

Allow AI-enabled deployments only where required.

Improve scalability of ML workloads.

Enable faster AI feature rollout.

Support differentiated AI-based pricing models.

Acceptance Criteria

ML service can be deployed independently.

Surbo instance can run without ML service when not required.

OCR, Generative AI, and Intent Detection are accessible via APIs.

Existing functionality remains unaffected.

Deployment pipeline supports optional ML service activation.

initiatives.title: Implement front end assitance for Redis API- BOTSUP - 13899
initiatives.description:
We need a Redis API config screen where we can list all the apis with different keys and enable disable them from there.
These API updates key in the Reddis DB.

Current scenario- We have to call these APIs manually everytime we want to update the keys in DB.
We do not have a provision to manage them in front end.

Requirement- We want to establish these APIs in front end so that they can be accessed anytime. User should be able to enable and disable them.
Once enabled/disbaled their status will remain same untill it is changed again.
These two Keys are independent and does not impacts each others behaviour. They both can be in enable state parallely.

UI- There will be a new section just below the Config > Account in Admin account only.
New section’s name- Redis API Config
Currently We will list two Keys only- 
dont_use_external_link_async_queue
dont_use_chatbot_to_channel_async_queue

Below mentioned parameters are required for Listing-
DB - It will show which db is being impacted.
Key- It will show these keys - 
dont_use_external_link_async_queue
dont_use_chatbot_to_channel_async_queue
Description- It should show this- Value 0 means Assync, Value 1 means Sync

initiatives.title: Improve Error Handling and Validation Messages During Assistant Creation in Hub - 13993
initiatives.description:
While creating an Assistant in the Hub account, the current UI displays inaccurate or unclear error messages in certain scenarios. These messages are not user-friendly and can create confusion for users during assistant creation.

We need to improve the error handling and validation messages so that users receive clear and actionable feedback when an error occurs.


1. Duplicate Assistant Name

Current Behavior

When a user tries to create a new assistant using an existing assistant name, the UI shows an unclear error message:
Object: object

Expected Behavior

The system should display a clear error message:

Error: Duplicate name

2. Incorrect Model Selection

Current Behavior

When a user selects an incorrect or unsupported model during assistant creation, the system returns the following error:

500: Internal Server Error

Expected Behavior
 The UI should display a meaningful error message such as:

Error: Please choose the correct model

3. Assistant Gets Saved Despite Error

Current Behavior

In the incorrect model scenario, even after displaying the 500 error, the assistant still gets saved with inactive Status

Expected Behavior

The assistant should not be saved if the selected model is invalid.

initiatives.title: Enable passing of mobile number parameter in Master Intent for RCS channel - 14024
initiatives.description:
Currently, in the bot builder, Master Intent supports passing user details (mobile number and name) for WhatsApp channel using predefined SRB parameters (e.g., WhatsApp number and WhatsApp name).

However, for the RCS channel, this functionality is not available.

Current Limitation:

In Master Intent → User Response field → Dropdown, only intents are visible.

There is no option to select mobile number parameters for RCS.

Requirement

We want to enhance Master Intent functionality for RCS channel to allow passing of user details.

Enhancement:

When user clicks on User Response field:

Dropdown should include:

Available intents (existing behavior)

Mobile Number parameter



Field

Value

User Number

SRB_1





Current Behavior

Only intents are visible in the dropdown.

No option to pass mobile number or name for RCS channel.

Feature exists for WhatsApp but missing for RCS.

Expected Behavior

Dropdown in User Response field should show:

Intents

SRB_1 → User Number

Users should be able to select these parameters and pass them in Master Intent.

This should be applicable for Geolocation Master as well.

Note- This should be common in Whatsapp and RCS.
For whatsapp we are updating icon and field name.

comments.text: 🎯 Target date was set to Apr 1, 2026

initiatives.title: Validation Failure handling | Phase 2 | Botsup - 13929
initiatives.description:
Currently, invalid input handling is not supported at the intent level for WA Flow, rating and product catelogue intents in both Surbo and Botsup. When a user enters an invalid input in these intents, there is no mechanism to redirect them further.

We need to enhance Botsup with intent-level validation failure handling for these intents. Surbo will not have a UI for this feature; however, the functionality will be mapped at the backend to ensure compatibility.

In Botsup: Full support and UI for setting up validation failure handling. UI can be copied from rest of the intents as this is already implemented there.

In Surbo: No UI changes, but backend mapping will ensure existing bots work with validation failure settings. If a Surbo user edits such an intent, they can modify other functionalities but cannot edit the validation failure settings, which will remain intact.

Scope of Change:

Support invalid input handling for:

WA Flow

Rating

Product catelogue

Provide option to redirect user to a defined path or intent when invalid input is given.

Enable validation failure support in Botsup UI under each of the above intents.

Surbo will not expose this feature in UI but will retain backend compatibility.

Redirection handling for validation failure should be implemented in the same way as currently available in the Date intent.

Scenarios to Handle:

If user enters invalid input  → Redirect as per configuration.

If validation failure settings exist (configured via Botsup) and user opens the intent in Surbo:

Validation failure configuration remains intact.

User can update other parts of the intent.

User cannot edit or remove validation failure settings from Surbo UI.

initiatives.title: Email Integration for Karix - 13901
initiatives.description:
Platform- BOTSUP
Instance- India

We need to integrate Email configurations for KARIX platform. There are several emails which are being triggered from the onboarding to BOt creation.
1)Welcome mail- This mail triggers when we onboard a new user in the platform
Current functionality - When Admin creates a new account using mobile number and email id, This email gets triggered on that particular email id.
Current Subject-Welcome to Botsup!
New required content- 
Change Subject from “Welcome to Botsup“ to “Welcome“
Change Botsup to Karix from everywhere it is written in the current mail.
Remove Activate Plan button.
Remove this line “In case of any support write to surbosupport@vfirst.com“

2)OTP Verification mail- This mail triggers when user logged into the account and try for mail verification.
Current functionality - When user logged into the account and clicks on “click here“ for email verification. We trigger an otp through the mail to the user.
Current Subject-Confirm your Email | Botsup
New required content- 
Remove Botsup from the subject “Confirm your Email | Botsup“
Remove “Welcome to Botsup.“ from the content.
Remove “Yours, Team Botsup from the content”

3)Reset Password- This mail triggers when user tries to reset the password.
Current Subject- Confirm your Email | Botsup
New required content- 
Update the subject with- Reset your password!
Remove “Welcome to Botsup.“ from the content.
Remove “Yours, Team Botsup from the content”

4)Report recieved mail- This mail triggers when user downloads the report. They recieve this report through the mail.
Current Subject- Daily Report | Chatbot
New required content- 
Remove “In case of any query get in touch with us at surbosupport@vfirst.com“ this from the content.
Remove “Yours, Team Botsup from the content”

5)Team mate Addition- This mail triggers when user adds a team mate account. This mail goes to the team mate user.
Current Subject- You have been invited to join Botsup
New required content- 
Remove Botsup from subject “You have been invited to join Botsup“
Remove “In case of any query get in touch with us at surbosupport@vfirst.com“ this from the content.
Remove “Yours, Team Botsup from the content”
Remove Botsup from this line “You have been invited to join the team by botsup@vfirst.com on Botsup“

6)Plan expiry- This email triggers in a specific time period when plan is about to expire.
Current Subject- Chatbot Subscription Expiring soon!
New required content- 
Remove “In case of any query get in touch with us at surbosupport@vfirst.com“ this from the content.
Remove “Yours, Team Botsup from the content”
Remove “With Regards,
Team Valuefirst“

Note- Remove Logo from all the emails.
Content should be in plain text. No bold letters should be there.
I have mentioned all the current subject lines of all the mails for the reference. All the mails can be checked in the mailtrap login
All other emails should not be integrated for Karix.

initiatives.title: Manage Team mate screen optimisation for Karix| FE - 13922
initiatives.description:
Instance- India
Platform- Botsup- Karix

Once we create and onboard a WABA account in Botsup, that user can login and add team mates uner its account so that team mates can also access the account.
While adding team mate there are multiple permissions which can be enabled/disabled as per the role.
In current scenario, there are 



analyst

creator

editor

test1

viewer

these many roles are visible and so many permissions are also visible according to these roles.

For Karix we need only creator as a role and few permissions only.
Users should not be able to add/remove any permission.
user can only see what all permissions are being assigned to the creator.
These will be fixed permissions.

Permissions needs to be removed-
Remove all the permission from front end which has Infinito and subscription in it.
Remove all the permission related to Razorpay.
Remove permission of Make payment(Bayan Pay)
Remove permission of Add team mate.
Remove permission of View invoice.
Remove permission of View plan.

In UI only creator should be visible and his allowed permissions should be visible only.
Rest all the permissions should be removed.
If any of the allowed permission has above mentioned “words“ - remove them.

initiatives.title: Handle html tags in BOT report | Analytics - 13923
initiatives.description:
Instance- India Platform- Surbo, Botsup Channels- Whatsapp, web, Rcs Current scenario- While creating the bot, we use few html tags for text formatting. To bold the text we write Hello To format the sentence in next line we use
We use/write these tags in front end of Intents. Problem statement- When we download reports , we get all the textual data selected/entered by users in the journey. In case where we are diplaying list/options to the end user in the child intent of an API and using html tags for formatting. When user selects any option in the journey and then we download the report, we can see html tags as well in the reports. For analysis and data clarity , html tags should not be visible in the reports. Proposed solution- We should give a provision to the user/analyst to choose intents while downloading the report. Those selected intents will hide html in the reports. If user/analyst selects 5 intents in the report which consists html tags, then report should not show html tags in those intent’s data. For UI- https://xd.adobe.com/view/72c84379-6f8e-4951-bdf6-6b9ba2e5fecc-279b/ There will be a drop down “Choose intents to hide HTML“ This drop down will show the list of all intents exist in the selected bot. There will be a select all option also, so that all the intents can be selected in a single click. This field should be optional, means if user does not select any intents still he should be able to download report. This should be applicable in two sections- 1) Download report under detail report section. 2) Scgedule new report under Report scheduler

initiatives.title: Integrate Karix RCM APIs with Surbo DIY (WhatsApp) - 13726
initiatives.description:
Integrate Karix RCS (Rich Communication Services) APIs with the Surbo Chatbot for WhatsApp Channel to enable rich, interactive messaging capabilities including multimedia content, actions, and enhanced user engagement through WhatsApp channel.

Objectives

Enable Surbo Chatbot to send and receive messages via Karix APIs

Implement rich messaging features (images, videos, carousels, list, button replies, etc.)

Ensure reliable message delivery and tracking

Scope

This epic covers the complete integration between Karix RCM APIs and Surbo Chatbot, including:

API authentication and authorization

Message sending and receiving functionality

Rich media handling (images, videos, files)

Interactive elements (List, button, flows, actions, carousels)

Delivery status tracking and webhooks

Success Criteria

All Karix RCM API endpoints successfully integrated

All test cases passed



Document Link: https://documenter.getpostman.com/view/19789335/UzR1M3Tj 

Please note: All the existing Generic WhatsApp channel supported features, validations and redirection logics should work as it is currently working with Unified and XML APIs.

initiatives.title: Technical Analysis :WebEx Wrapper Integration (JLR) - 13927
initiatives.description:
The client (JLR) requires a direct integration between SURBO and their custom WebEx Wrapper (Webhook) for live agent handover, bypassing the existing Genesis infrastructure.

Initial review of the client's webhook schema indicates a gap in handling multi-part attachments and transcript synchronization. We need to define the bidirectional communication contract to ensure seamless session management and media transfer.

Technical Requirements & Gap Analysis

1. Handover Flow (SURBO → Client Webhook)

Trigger: Live Agent Node in SURBO journey.

Required Data: * Full conversation transcript (JSON/URL).

User metadata (Identifier, Region, Language).

Media Support: The client's current endpoint fails to receive attachments. We must define a standard attachments[] array for their wrapper to consume.

2. Agent Response Flow (Client → SURBO)

Endpoint: SURBO External Agent API.

Attachment Handling: The client must provide a public URL for any media sent by the agent. SURBO will then deliver this to the end-user channel.

3. Session Management (Crucial)

State Persistence: The wrapper must maintain the conversation_id mapping.

Termination Signal: The client API must send a Close_Session event to SURBO to trigger the post-chat/feedback journey.



Please check the feasibity and Efforts required to deliver this.

initiatives.title: Structured responses -13857
initiatives.description:
We need to evaluate the technical requirements for SIA to support Structured Messaging across WhatsApp, RCS and other channels. Currently, our LLM agents primarily output text; we need a standardized way to trigger rich UI elements that these channels support.


Scope of Analysis

Schema Definition: Design a channel-agnostic JSON schema that the LLM can generate (or a post-processor can interpret) to represent:

Quick Replies / Buttons: Max 3 for WhatsApp, up to 10 for RCS.

List Messages: Vertical menus for larger selections.

MCQ Options: Mapping LLM "choices" to interactive UI elements.

LLM Prompt Engineering: Determine if the LLM should output the raw JSON structure or if we should use a "Tool/Function Calling" approach where the LLM calls a send_rich_message function.

Channel Parity & Fallbacks: * Identify "least common denominator" features.

Define fallback behavior (e.g., if a channel doesn't support a specific button type, revert to numbered text list).

State Management: How to handle the user's "button click" (Postback) when it returns to the LLM. Does the agent treat it as a new user message?

Acceptance Criteria

Documented mapping of WhatsApp vs. RCS rich message constraints (character limits, button counts).

Proposed JSON schema for the SIA internal messaging layer.

Prototype prompt/function definition for the LLM to trigger a "List" message.

Strategy for handling "Postback" data (what happens when the user clicks)

Technical Considerations for Analysis

Character Limits: Channels have character limits, If your LLM generates a 50-character response option, the integration will break unless you have a truncation or "text-fallback" strategy.

The "Payload" vs "Label": Buttons have a Label (what the user sees) and a Payload (what the system sees). Your analysis should define how the LLM assigns a unique ID to a choice so that when the user clicks "Yes," the LLM knows which "Yes" they clicked in the conversation history.

initiatives.title: Enhance Reports Data Flow in Infinito - 13691
initiatives.description:
Currently, in Infinito, when users navigate to the Reports section, the system fetches analytics data from the Botsup database. 

We need to enhance the reporting architecture so that Infinito reports ultimately fetch data from the Surbo database, with Botsup acting as a proxy layer.

Current Behavior

User navigates to Reports section in Infinito.

Infinito sends a request to Botsup.

Botsup fetches report data directly from its own database.

Proposed Solution (Enhancement)

Update the reporting data flow as follows:

Infinito will continue to call Botsup for reports.

Botsup will act as a proxy layer.

Instead of querying its own database, Botsup will:

Fetch the required report data from the Surbo database

Return the response back to Infinito

This ensures that Surbo remains the single source of truth for analytics data.

initiatives.title: Audit Logs X Infinito DIY - 14018
initiatives.description:
Current Problem:

On Infinito DIY Chatbot, we don’t have any activity logs for compliances and to view change logs.

Requirement:

We require a separate module named “Audit Logs” under the Home for all users at the last place. 

This module will be based on permission and can be provided to any user based on RBAC (permission details are mentioned under Important Points to be considered as 8th & 9th point.

It will be available for the Admin user(Infinito Chatbot Admin and Botsup Admin), Parent User & Teammate user.

Admin logs should be visible to Admin only, not to any other users.

Instances:

Botsup

Infinito

Audit Log Module:

For the Admin-only audit log dashboard, show a Business User dropdown with search functionality to select the org. Post selection, the page table will be refreshed with user IDs.

Search Bar: Will match the keyword with all the logs (based on selected filters and date range) and fetch all the logs having the exact keyword. (For admin, it will only work post bussiness user selection).

User drop-down: Show all users along with the “All Users”(default) option. It will filter the data based on the selected user or all users (for admin it will work when BU is selected).

Data range selector: Filter the logs based on the date range (default 3 days, maximum selection 15 days).

Module selection drop-down: Filter the result based on selected module, i.e. All Module (default), User, Chatbot, Integrations, Settings & Analytics (for admin it will work when BU is selected).

Bot selection drop-down: Shows the associated bots under the account, with the Select Bot (default) option (for admin it will work when BU is selected).

A go button, when clicked, loads the updated logs based on the selected filter. 

Implement pagination and show 15 logs per page.

By default, selected values: 

Users/ Fields

Bussiness User

User

Date Range

Module

Bot

Admin

Show All users

All User (Post BU selection)

latest 3 dates (Post BU selection)

All Modules (Post BU selection)

Select Bot (Post BU selection)

Parent User

Don't Show

All User

select latest 3 dates

All Modules

Select Bot

Team Mate

Don't Show

TeamMate_ID

select latest 3 dates

All Modules

Select Bot

** By default, the Admin screen will be blank, but once a business user is selected, show the latest 3 days of logs and enable all of the remaining filters. Parent User will see the latest 15 logs, Teammate User will see their own latest 15 logs.

Content of the table of audit-related details:

Username: The user who made the changes

Action In: Flow, Login, Setting, Bot, Integration, Analytics, etc.

Bot ID: ID of the respective Bot, and in case of login, Analytics & settings, integration, mention NA.

IP Address: IP address of the user who made the changes.

Date & Time: Date & Time of the changes/ login.

Action: Bot Saved as draft, Bot Publish, Version saved, Flow Reset, Login, Report Scheduled, Sandbox Enabled, User created on Botsup, etc (incoporate all the possible actions). Please let the product know if any clarification needed for this point.

View Icon: It will open a pop-up showing old and new JSON with a copy button for both logs.



Important Points to be considered:



Admin logs will only be visible to the admin only.

Temp Pass generation, changes using the temp pass will be visible to admin only.

A duplicate of the bot will only be visible to the admin with both bot IDs.

Teammates can see their own logs only.

Every single change should be incorporated: Incorporate all the logs, including creation, edit, delete, configuration of every element of the bot, setting, reporting, integration and login, etc accross all chanels.

Under login logs, shows the login & logout session details.

Logs will be maintained based on the existing data retention policy, the latest 6 months logs on the dashboard and 5 years on the archive.

For Parent user permission will be given through Manage user on Admin on Infinito and Botsup.

For Teammates user, permission will be given through Users>> Teammates>> Permissions>> Audit Log (known as Activity Log).

Attachments:

Design Link

Navigation of Permission to teammate user

Navigation of Permission to parent user

Future Scope:

Audit log implementation on Wisely & Karix.

Design link: https://xd.adobe.com/view/1b3eda8d-0d57-4f29-9ab1-b744b4f7e493-4e6d/

initiatives.title: Knowledge Base - Web Crawling Feature - 14023
initiatives.description:
Module: AI Agent Library>Knowledge Base


1. Feature Overview

The Web Crawling feature enables users on the Surbo DIY platform to teach their AI agent by pointing it at a website. The system automatically crawls the website, reads its pages, extracts content and links, indexes the information, and makes it available for the AI agent to reference when answering end-user queries.

This is a zero-configuration, consumer-grade experience. Users do not need to understand sitemaps, APIs, or HTML. The entire flow is guided, self-service, and non-destructive — users can add, refresh, or remove website sources at any time.

1.1  Problem Statement

Today, users must manually upload docs into their AI agent's knowledge base. This is:

Time-consuming for websites with more than a few pages

Prone to staleness — content goes out of date as the website changes

Technically inaccessible for non-developer users

1.2  Proposed Solution

Introduce an automated web crawling pipeline accessible through a guided UI with the following end-to-end flow:

User enters a website URL and optional configuration

System crawls the site up to the specified depth and refresh schedule

Crawled pages are indexed and stored per KB source

User can browse, filter, and preview individual crawled pages

User can attach the crawled pages with the AI agent in the intent.

AI agent references indexed content when answering queries

2. User Stories

Persona

I want to…

So that…

As a platform user

add my company website as a knowledge source without technical help

my AI chatbot can answer customer questions using up-to-date content from my site

As a platform user

see exactly which pages were crawled and what content was extracted

I can verify my AI has the right information and troubleshoot incorrect answers

As a platform user

manually trigger a refresh of a website source

I can update my AI's knowledge immediately after a major site update

As a platform user

remove a crawled page or an entire website source

I can keep my AI's knowledge base accurate and focused

3. Screens & UI States

3.1  Knowledge Base — Web Crawling Tab

This is the landing screen for all web-crawling sources. It has two states:

State

Behaviour

Empty State

Shown when no web sources have been added yet.

Displays a floating illustration, headline "No websites connected yet", a short plain-English description of the feature, and a primary CTA button "Add New". The illustration gently floats  to draw attention without being distracting.

List State

Shown once one or more sources exist. Displays a card grid of all website sources. Each card shows: source name, Web links count, size, URL, and actions (Edit and Delete).

An "Add New" button is persistently visible in the top-right corner.

3.2  Add Website Form

Accessed via the "Add New" or "Add WebLink" button. A single-step form presented in a card. No multi-step wizard — all fields are visible at once to reduce cognitive overhead for non-technical users.

3.3  Detail View (Crawled Pages List)

Accessed by clicking a website source card. Displays a header stats bar followed by a searchable, filterable table of all crawled pages. Clicking the eye icon on any row opens a content drawer. Clicking the trash/delete icon deletes the particular crawled link. Clicking the refresh icon fetches the lates data of that specific web link and updates the crawled data.

Stats bar elements:
a) Website Link : The link that has been fetched
b) Weblinks crawled : Count of the links crawled
c) Total size : Aggregated size of the links crawled
d) Depth Level : The level of depth the website has been crawled
e) Refresh Schedule: Frequency of the web crawling that has been set
f) Status : If linked with any agent in a bot or not (Linked/Not Linked)

Table columns: Refer section 4.2

3.4  Content Drawer
A slide-in panel from the right showing the extracted text content of a single page plus all outbound links found on that page. Allows users to verify what their AI will learn from each page without leaving the platform and the content on this page should be editable by the user.

4. Field Definitions & Behaviour

All fields use plain-English labels targeted at non-technical users. Technical terms are hidden behind tooltips (? icons). The table below defines every input field in the Add Website form.

4.1  Add Website Form Fields

Field Label

Type

Plain-English Description

Validation & Behaviour

What should we call this source?

Text input

A internal name for this website source. Used as the card title and in the detail view header. Not visible to end users of the AI chatbot.

Required. Max 60 characters. Character counter shown. Inline error on empty submit: "Please give this a name." Input highlights red on error.

Description

Text input

Optional memo visible only to the platform user. Helps identify the source in large knowledge bases. The AI does not use this field.

No max length enforced in UI (backend may truncate at 255 chars). Placeholder: "e.g. Official product help pages."

Website address (URL)

URL input

The starting URL the crawler will visit. This is the entry point — all crawling begins here and follows links outward from this page.

Required. Must begin with http:// or https://. Real-time validation: border turns red and inline error appears if format is invalid. On valid entry, error clears immediately. Hint text: "Paste the full link, e.g. https://support.mycompany.com".

Read the entire website automatically

Toggle (on/off)

When enabled, the crawler will attempt to follow all discovered links regardless of depth. When disabled, the Depth selector below controls how many levels deep the crawler goes.

Default: OFF. When toggled ON, the Depth selector (how deep should we go?)is visually dimmed and disabled (pointer-events: none). Toggle state persists within the session. Tooltip: "Enable to let us explore all pages automatically."

How deep should we go?

Number input

E.g. controls the crawl depth — how many levels of links the crawler follows from the starting URL. Level 1 = homepage only. Level 2 = homepage + all pages it links to directly. Level 3 = one level deeper still. And so on.

Default: 1. Selected state highlighted. Disabled and dimmed when "Read entire website" toggle is ON. Hint text updates per selection with plain-English context. Tooltip: "Level 1 = homepage only. Level 2 = homepage + pages it links to."

How often should we check for updates?

Dropdown

Controls how frequently the system re-crawls the website to pick up new or changed content. Ensures the AI's knowledge stays current without manual intervention.

Default: Never. Options: "Every day", "Every week", "Every month", “Custom”.Mutually exclusive chips. 
Custom: Input Number of days

4.2  Crawled Pages Table Columns

Column

What it means

Behaviour

S.No.

Sequential row number within this website source.

Auto-generated. Resets to 1 per source.

Add to KB

Whether the crawled web link needs to be used in the KB of the Agent

If Checked (Default), the crawled link will be add to the Agent’s knowledge base (RAG).

ID

A short unique identifier for the crawled page record within the system. Used for debugging and API references.

System-generated. Displayed in a monospace code chip. Not editable by the user.

URL

The full web address of the crawled page. Clicking opens the page in a new browser tab so the user can compare the live page to the extracted content.

Click to copy option appears on mouse hover and URL gets copied to clipboard on click.

Last Updated

The date and time the system last successfully extracted and indexed content from this page.

Tooltip: "When we last successfully read this page." Format: D Mon, HH:MM AM/PM IST. Never shows future dates.

Next Refresh

The scheduled date and time of the next automatic re-crawl for this page, based on the refresh frequency set by the user at the source level.

Tooltip: "When we'll check this page for changes next." Shows "—" if refresh frequency is set to Manual. Format matches Last Updated.

Size

The size of the extracted and stored text content for this page, measured in kilobytes. Gives the user a sense of how much content the has been indexed from each page.

Tooltip: "How much content was read from this page." Displayed as "X KB" or "X MB" (auto-scaled). Does not include raw HTML — reflects clean extracted text size only.

Actions

Row-level action buttons: View Content (eye icon), Manual Refresh (Refresh icon), Remove Page (bin icon).

Remove triggers a confirmation dialog.

5. Interaction & Behaviour Specification

5.1  Form Validation

Source Name: Required. Shows inline error "Please give this a name." on submit if empty. Input border turns red.

Website URL: Required. Real-time validation on input change — error shown immediately if URL does not start with http:// or https://. Error clears as soon as the user types a valid format.

All other fields have defaults and are not required.

The form does not crawls until all required fields are valid. 

5.2  Progress Toast

After the user clicks "Start Reading Website", a toast notification appears in the bottom-right corner showing real-time crawl progress. The toast progresses through four labelled stages:

Progress

Stage Label

Sub-text shown

0–20%

Connecting to website…

Checking if the site is accessible

20–50%

Reading pages…

Scanning content and links

50–75%

Processing content…

We are learning from the pages

100%

All done! 🎉

"[Source Name]" is ready — click the card to explore

The progress bar turns green at 100%. The icon changes from an hourglass to a green checkmark. The toast auto-dismisses. The Submit button shimmer-animates during processing and resets to its default state after completion. The view transitions to the List State 800ms after 100% is reached.

5.3  Search & Filter (Detail View)

Search input filters the pages table in real time as the user types. Matches against ID and URL (case-insensitive).

If no pages match the filters, an empty state message is shown in the table body: "No pages match your search. Try a different keyword or filter."

5.4  Delete & Remove Behaviour

Removing a website source (bin icon on card): shows a browser confirm dialog "Remove \"[Name]\" from your knowledge base?" Confirmed deletion removes the card and all associated crawled page data. If no sources remain, the view reverts to Empty State.

Removing an individual page (bin icon on table row): shows an inline confirmation. Removing a page removes it from the index — the AI can no longer reference it.

6. Acceptance Criteria

ID

Criterion

Priority

AC-01

User can navigate to Knowledge Base > Web Crawling tab and see an empty state with a CTA button when no sources exist.

Must

AC-02

User can open the Add Website form and see all fields with plain-English labels and tooltip icons.

Must

AC-03

Source Name field shows an inline error on empty submit attempt.

Must

AC-04

URL field shows a real-time inline error for invalid URL formats and clears it when the user corrects the input.

Must

AC-05

"Read entire website" toggle disables the Depth input field when turned on and re-enables it when turned off.

Must

AC-06

Depth input field hint text updates to reflect the selected level in plain English.

Should

AC-07

Submit button triggers a progress toast with at minimum 3 visible stage transitions before showing 100% completion.

Must

AC-08

After successful submission, the view transitions to the List State and the new source card is visible.

Must

AC-09

Each source card correctly displays  web link count, size, and URL.

Must

AC-10

Clicking a source card navigates to the Detail View showing the pages table.

Must

AC-11

Pages table displays all columns with correct data, tooltips on applicable headers.

Must

AC-12

Content drawer opens from the right with slide animation when the eye icon is clicked.

Must

AC-13

Drawer shows page , URL, size, last updated, extracted content, and outbound links.

Must

AC-14

Drawer closes when the overlay or ✕ or close button is clicked.

Must

AC-15

Search input filters pages table in real time against id and URL.

Must

AC-16

Empty filter state message shown when no pages match the active filters.

Should

AC-17

Deleting a source card shows a confirmation dialog and removes the card and all associated pages on confirm.

Must

AC-18

Deleting the last source card transitions the view back to Empty State.

Must

AC-19

"Refresh Now" button in the Detail View header triggers a re-crawl and updates button text to loading state during processing.

Should

 7. Out of Scope 

Authentication-gated pages (pages behind a login wall)

8. Technical Notes for Engineering

Crawl jobs should be queued and processed asynchronously. The frontend polls for status updates — or uses WebSocket/SSE for real-time progress.

Implement crawl rate limiting per domain to avoid being blocked or causing load on the target server.

Store extracted content as clean text along with the crawled images in the KB index.

Page size reported in the UI should reflect clean extracted text size.

"Next Refresh" timestamps should be stored in UTC and converted to the user's timezone (Asia/Kolkata IST for the current deployment) on the frontend.

Source-level delete should cascade to all associated crawled page records.

The system should handle crawl errors gracefully — mark the page as "Error" status and continue crawling other pages rather than failing the entire job.

initiatives.title: Enhance Master Intent with Tag Selection Support and Response Saving via Session Variables - 14021
initiatives.description:
Platform- Infinto/Botsup
Instance- India, KSA, Indo
Channel- Whatsapp, Web, RCS

Currently, in the Master Intent (Excel / Geolocation), the User Response field under the “Search Variable” section only allows values to be passed through:

Previous intent responses

Manual entry using syntax like: {{SESSION:TAG}}

There is no direct UI support to select session tags, intent tags.

Additionally, there is no provision to store the response data fetched from the master sheet, limiting reuse of this data across the bot journey.

Problem Statement

1. Limited Tag Usage in User Response Field

Users cannot select session variables or tags directly from UI

Manual syntax ({{SESSION:TAG}}) is required

2. No Option to Save Master Intent Response

Master intent fetches data from Excel/Geo sheets

Retrieved data cannot be stored for reuse

Analysts cannot use this data in:

Other intents

API parameters

Proposed Solution

1. Tag Selection Support in User Response Field

Enhancement

Add a Tag Icon/Button below the User Response field in the Search Variable section

Behavior

On clicking the tag icon:

A dropdown should appear

It should display:

Session variables

This dropdown should behave similar to other intents (e.g  text intent)

Expected Outcome

Users can easily select variables instead of writing manual syntax

2. Add “Save Response in Bot” Section

Enhancement

Introduce a new section similar to API intent:

Save Response in Bot

Behavior

When enabled:

System should allow users to map Excel column values to session variables

User should be able to:

Define session variable names

Map each variable to a column from the master sheet

Example

Excel Columns:

Name

Account Number

Branch

User Configuration:

user_name → Name

account_number → Account Number

Data Handling

If multiple values are returned for a column:

Store them in comma-separated format

Example:

Account Numbers → 12345, 67890, 45678

Session Variable Behavior

Created session variables should:

Be accessible across the entire bot journey

Be visible in all tag-supported intents

Be selectable via tag dropdowns

Integration with API Intent

These session variables should be usable directly in:

API parameters under session variable

Use cases to be considered-
User is able to to pass session variables in tag values in the master intent.
User is able to save excel data in session variables.
User is able to pass this session variable in further API intent and other intents.
User is able to pass this session variable in further Master intent.

initiatives.title: 14029 - Allow Dynamic URL (Tag-Based) Support in Rich Card “Visit Website” Button (Master Intent Integration)
initiatives.description:
While configuring a Master Intent (Excel-based) and (geo location based) and using its response to populate a Rich Card Intent, there is a limitation in passing dynamic URLs to the “Visit Website” button.

In this use case, the URL is stored in an Excel column of the Master Intent and is expected to be passed dynamically using a tag format such as:

{{URLfield}}

However, currently the frontend validation restricts saving the bot, throwing an error:

Invalid URL in cards



Problem Statement

Rich Card “Visit Website” button requires a valid static URL

Dynamic values (tags like {{URLfield}}) are not accepted

UI validation blocks saving the bot

This functionality is supported in Surbo (legacy platform) but not working in the current system

Expected Behavior

System should allow dynamic URL values using tags (e.g., {{URLfield}})


Proposed Solution

Update validation logic to:

Allow tag-based values in URL field

Skip strict URL validation when dynamic tags are used

Ensure backend supports resolving dynamic URL at runtime

This should be implemented for both excel and geolocation based Master.


Note- there might be a case where user enters randomly in {{}}. For such cases we should not block him from saving the intent. We are expecting error in such case at the run time.
We can think of an alternate for this in later stage.
We might implement validations only for static invalid URLs in future.
